feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: Even with billboards across the region, a Mount Washington man’s search for a kidney continues
entry.summary: False

entry.title: A new Ebola outbreak has already killed 87 people in Democratic Republic of Congo
entry.summary: Health officials are working to contain a new Ebola outbreak in the DRC of a virus strain with no known vaccine.

entry.title: Scientists find a remarkable molecule that could help humans live up to 200 years cancer free
entry.summary: One answer for how bowhead whales live to be 200 years old with no signs of cancer was recently identified as a protein called CIRBP.

entry.title: Here Are 3 Things You Can Actually Do To Lower Your Risk Of Colorectal Cancer
entry.summary: As told by a medical oncologist.

entry.title: France says cruise ship Andes virus matches known South American viruses
entry.summary: France’s Pasteur Institute has fully sequenced the Andes virus found in a French passenger from the MV Hondius cruise ship. They discovered it matches viruses already known in South America. Health Minister Stéphanie Rist said Friday on X that there's no evid…

entry.title: My 29-year-old son had a stomach ache that wouldn't go away. He was diagnosed with stage 4 colon cancer and died 6 months later.
entry.summary: Kathy Lemoine's 30-year-old son, Andrew Reaster, died after he was diagnosed with stage 4 colon cancer. His main symptom was a stomach ache.

entry.title: Hantavirus latest updates: Suspected case at New York high school not linked to cruise ship
entry.summary: There are no known cases of hantavirus in the United States, health officials say.

entry.title: Beyond Pain Relief: Scientists Discover a Protein That Could Stop Osteoarthritis in Its Tracks
entry.summary: Researchers have identified the SHP protein as a key regulator that suppresses cartilage-degrading enzymes and slows osteoarthritis progression.

entry.title: 10 High-Protein Snacks for a Diabetes-Friendly Diet
entry.summary: These high-protein snack recipes are low in saturated fat and sodium to fit seamlessly into a diabetes-friendly eating pattern.

entry.title: 5 foods to support strong bones
entry.summary: Eating the right nutrients can help you in the fight to maintain bone mass as you age. These foods are high in what you need.

entry.title: Your Body Clock May Enhance The Benefits of Exercise, Study Finds
entry.summary: While some people can spring out of bed at six in the morning and go straight into their day, others prefer to wake up later as they're most productive in the afternoon or evening.

entry.title: I Know Way Too Much About Infectious Diseases. Here’s Why I’m Considering Taking a Cruise With My Family Anyway.
entry.summary: Are they unique hotbeds of illness? Let’s look at the research.

entry.title: When should you get a mammogram? Conflicting advice makes it hard to know
entry.summary: Deciding when to get routine mammograms is confusing.

entry.title: What Americans can learn from South Koreans on longevity
entry.summary: There is a lot the United States can learn about health and longevity from South Korea.

entry.title: Golden Retriever Puppy Is Scared of Little Boy but Comforts Him When He Falls
entry.summary: This sweet Golden Retriever puppy comforted a little boy after he fell, and viewers cannot get over the adorable first playdate.

entry.title: Scientists find hidden brain nutrient deficit that may fuel anxiety
entry.summary: A major analysis of brain scans found that people with anxiety disorders have noticeably lower levels of choline, a nutrient crucial for healthy brain function. The strongest evidence appeared in the prefrontal cortex, the region tied to emotional control and…

entry.title: Map Reveals Cities Facing Worst Mosquito Swarms This Summer
entry.summary: Mosquito season is coming around, and this year it could be particularly bad for many Americans living in some of the biggest cities.

entry.title: A hospital worker felt healthy. Then he had 3 heart attacks in 4 days: "What have I done to myself?"
entry.summary: Tommy Bell thought he was in good shape. But a series of heart attacks painted a more complicated picture.

entry.title: Mesa sees largest measles outbreak in decades, health officials urge vaccination
entry.summary: Public health officials in Maricopa County say they are confronting the most significant measles outbreak the region has seen in 30 years, with 15 confirmed cases so far in 2026.

entry.title: How Much You Need To Walk Every Day To Cut Your Risk Of Heart Disease
entry.summary: Data suggests there's a certain number of minutes you should aim for to improve your cardiovascular health. Here's how you can sneak them in.

entry.title: Disease, Drought, Climate Change — And the Joy of Fighting Back
entry.summary: On my Virginia farm, I have a front-row seat to the grim consequences of a warming planet. But I’m not surrendering.

entry.title: How weight loss jabs have changed the battle with obesity
entry.summary: More than 100 million people are on them worldwide, but how does the future of weight loss look?

entry.title: Scientists discover tiny gut particles that may drive aging and chronic disease
entry.summary: A new study suggests microscopic particles from the gut may actively drive inflammation and chronic diseases associated with aging. Remarkably, gut particles from young animals appeared to counter some aging-related damage in older animals, hinting at new pos…

entry.title: CDC tightens home monitoring guidance for high-risk hantavirus contacts
entry.summary: People in the “high-risk” category are instructed to stay home and limit contact with others, avoid being in buildings other than their home and avoid visitors to their residences. They are also supposed to coordinate any essential travel with their state or …

entry.title: Fourth King County resident potentially exposed to hantavirus
entry.summary: A fourth King County resident is under monitoring for the Andes type of hantavirus linked to the MV Hondius cruise ship.

entry.title: Fourth King County resident linked to cruise ship hantavirus outbreak
entry.summary: A fourth King County resident may have been exposed to hantavirus, and someone in Chelan County is infected with a different type of hantavirus.

entry.title: Health officials track fourth King County resident tied to MV Hondius Andes hantavirus
entry.summary: Public Health – Seattle & King County officials are monitoring a fourth King County resident for possible exposure to the Andes type of hantavirus linked to the

entry.title: Sin Nombre hantavirus case tied to home mouse exposure in Chelan County
entry.summary: Chelan-Douglas Health District officials have confirmed a case of hantavirus in a Chelan County resident, marking one of the occasional infections reported eac

entry.title: Hantavirus: US has no cases of Andes hantavirus
entry.summary: A flight carrying U.S. citizens who were on the ship arrived Monday in Nebraska.

entry.title: Someone in Chelan County tested positive for hantavirus
entry.summary: Someone has tested positive for hantavirus in Chelan County.

entry.title: This Common Type of Food May Be Raising Your Risk of Heart Disease
entry.summary: Heart experts say ultra-processed foods could be quietly fueling a global rise in heart disease and early death.

entry.title: 5 Standing Exercises That Flatten Apron Belly Faster Than Gym Workouts After 60
entry.summary: A trainer with 20+ years experience shares 5 standing moves that target apron belly after 60, no gym machines needed.

entry.title: 2 bitten by fox in Chapel Hill
entry.summary: A fox bit two people in Orange County on Friday, according to the county's animal services.

entry.title: Two Tiny Preemie Kittens Find Each Other Right When They Need It Most
entry.summary: “They may not be related by blood, but they certainly act like they are sister and brother."

entry.title: I'm A Spine Doctor. Here's 1 Thing I'd Never, Ever Do If I Had Back Pain.
entry.summary: This dangerous combination of movements is throwing out people’s backs regularly.

entry.title: People with ADHD have a natural inclination for intuitive thinking and creative breakthroughs, according to a new study
entry.summary: Researchers set out to test a long-running hunch among cognitive scientists that people with ADHD were more creative thinkers.

entry.title: CDC Didn’t Tell New York About Resident on Hantavirus-Plagued Cruise
entry.summary: There’s no indication that the New Yorker was returning to the U.S. But public health experts said the city and state still should have been informed.

entry.title: Connecticut health officials warn of hepatitis A outbreak linked to shellfish
entry.summary: The Connecticut Department of Public Health warned residents about a hepatitis A outbreak in New York that has been linked to shellfish.

entry.title: A 47-year study reveals when strength and fitness start to fade
entry.summary: A groundbreaking Swedish study that tracked people for nearly 50 years has revealed when the body’s physical decline quietly begins. Researchers found that fitness, strength, and muscle endurance start slipping around age 35, with the decline accelerating ove…

entry.title: How to get rid of mosquitoes and keep your home 100% bug free this year
entry.summary: I found the best way to get rid of mosquitoes after talking with pest control experts. They recommend the best products and methods for making your home mosquito-free.

entry.title: Scientists Say Cognitive Decline Isn’t Inevitable — Your Brain Can Improve at Any Age
entry.summary: A long-term study found that brain health can improve throughout life with consistent daily habits, cognitive training, and personalized support, regardless of age.

entry.title: Man, 53, Loses 100 Pounds and Gets Off Insulin With These 3 Lifestyle Changes
entry.summary: Jim Anders, 53, of Corpus Christi, Texas, thought feeling exhausted was part of getting older.

entry.title: Ebola outbreak with uncommon strain erupts in Congo and Uganda; 65 deaths
entry.summary: WHO learned of potential cases May 5; US CDC said it just heard about it yesterday.

entry.title: Harsh Parenting Biologically Distorts Child Stress Regulation
entry.summary: A new study uses heart monitors to prove that harsh parenting disrupts a child's biological stress co-regulation system.

entry.title: How Many Eggs Is Too Many to Eat Each Week? Nutrition Experts Explain
entry.summary: Nutrition experts explain how many eggs is too many to eat each week. They say eggs are a healthy food for most people and it's safe to eat seven to 14 eggs per week, depending on your age, medical conditions, and overall diet. Eating more could too much—they…

entry.title: Garlic Is a Secret Weapon Against Mosquitoes, Study Finds
entry.summary: Some mosquitoes and flies don't mate or lay eggs as much when exposed to a compound found in garlic.

entry.title: Scientists Studied Half A Million People And Found A Sleep Sweet Spot For Healthy Aging
entry.summary: Too little sleep was linked to faster ageing. So was too much.

entry.title: I Asked 2 Nutritionists If Sourdough Is Actually Healthier Than Other Bread, and They Both Had the Same Answer
entry.summary: The reality doesn't always match the hype.

entry.title: Cruise ships are perfect breeding ground for viruses, but we can’t get enough
entry.summary: Buffets and shared spaces help make cruise ships ideal places for viruses to flourish, disease experts say. Still, Americans are booking cruises in record numbers.

entry.title: Review of 60+ Alcohol-Caused Diseases Details Reversibility
entry.summary: A new study outlines the 60+ diseases caused by alcohol and examines which health harms are reversible.
